applyNote: Patches apply strictly in array order; afterStation refers to the route's stop order at application time (earlier patches in this array may have introduced it). afterStation START means the station becomes the first stop. routeIndex indexes the routes array of the serie entry. When add_stop promotes a station that is present in the route's via array, the applier should also remove it from via. rename_serie changes only the id. add_route creates the serie entry (with meta) if the id does not exist yet.

unexpressible.note: page 3 encodes RB51 and RB64 as two routes of entry 20200 and folds RB61 into entry 20350's single Hengelo-Bielefeld route, unlike pages 1, 2 and 4 which have separate RB51/RB64/RB61 entries; the convention text says one entry per drawn line with brand ids, but its Implied edits list only names the 20000 splits (pages 1 and 3) and says do not renumber the RB entries; whether page 3's 20200/20350 must also be split is not concluded in the verdicts document, so no patch is emitted; flagged for the maintainer
